title: Using GIS technology to map shelter allocation in Azraq refugee camp

indicators_measured.name: Number of refugees, by host country
Number of refugees per 100,000 population, by country of origin

indicators_targeted.name: Refugee population in host country
indicators_targeted.rationale: Efficient shelter allocation for ~26,000 Syrian refugees in 10,000 transitional shelters at Azraq camp

indicators_targeted.name: Refugee density
indicators_targeted.rationale: Managing high-volume daily refugee intake (300–1,600 newcomers/day) requires accurate shelter inventory

summary: At Azraq refugee camp in Jordan, UNHCR faced a shelter allocation bottleneck for ~26,000 Syrian refugees across 10,000 fixed transitional shelters with physical addresses. Associate Field Officer Yahya Hassune replaced a fragile shared Excel system with a custom GIS application enabling staff to click shelters on a camp map, view availability, occupancy, damage status, and informal occupation, allocating shelters in ~20 seconds and feeding data directly into progress tracking. The system reduced shelter allocation staff from 11 to 5 while processing up to 1,600 newcomers in a single day. Methods include geographic information systems, spatial database design, map-based interactive allocation, and real-time shelter inventory management. The innovation demonstrates how geospatial technology improves refugee camp operations, shelter access, and humanitarian response efficiency.
